result:
Done. Built and committed a persistent JSON REST API for the `notes` resource.

## What was built

**Zero-dependency Node.js (ESM)** using built-in `node:http` and `node:sqlite` (Node 24+):

- `src/db.js` â€” on-disk SQLite store (`data/notes.db`, overridable via `DB_PATH`). Creates the table if absent, with prepared statements for CRUD. Notes get a server-assigned UUID `id` and ISO-8601 `createdAt`.
- `src/server.js` â€” HTTP router on `PORT` (default 3000) with the full endpoint set and JSON validation.
- `test/api.test.js` â€” `node:test` suite (7 tests), including a real restart-persistence check.
- `package.json` â€” added `start` (`node src/server.js`) and `test` scripts.
- `README.md` / `.gitignore` â€” docs + ignore the runtime `data/` dir.

## Endpoints & behavior

| Endpoint | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `POST /notes` | Validates `title`+`body` are non-empty strings â†’ `201` + note; invalid â†’ `400` |
| `GET /notes` | `200` + array of all notes |
| `GET /notes/:id` | `200` + note, or `404` |
| `PUT /notes/:id` | Partial update (title and/or body, validated) â†’ `200`; invalid â†’ `400`; absent â†’ `404` |
| `DELETE /notes/:id` | `204`, or `404` |

## Verification
- All 7 tests pass, including **"notes survive a server restart"** (spawns server, creates a note, kills it, restarts, confirms the note is still readable).
- Independent curl smoke test against the default config confirmed 201/400/200/204 responses and persistence.

Committed as `bb9bf2c`. I staged only the new API files â€” the pre-existing `.agents/` deletions in the working tree were already there and left untouched.
